name: Low-Sugar Refrigerator Strawberry Jam
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan, combine sliced strawberries, sugar, and lemon juice. Heat for 5 minutes, crushing the berries slightly. Bring to a boil; boil rapidly, stirring constantly, for 3 minutes.
2. In a small bowl, sprinkle unflavored gelatin over cold water. Let stand for 1 minute. Add to strawberry mixture and heat, stirring until gelatin is completely dissolved, about 3 minutes.
3. Let jam stand for 5 minutes, skimming off foam with a spoon. Ladle into jars. Cover and cool slightly before storing in the refrigerator for several weeks or in the freezer for longer storage.
If cooked jam does not set within 24 hours after processing:
- The Department of Agriculture Home and Garden Bulletin No. 56 suggests recooking the jam using either powdered pectin or liquid pectin.
- To remake with powdered pectin: For each quart of jelly or jam, measure 1/2 cup sugar, 1/4 cup water, and 4 tsp powdered pectin. Mix pectin and water and bring to boiling, stirring constantly to prevent scorching. Add the jam and sugar. Stir thoroughly. Bring to a full rolling boil over high heat, stirring constantly. Boil mixture hard for 1/2 minute. Remove from heat; skim off foam. Ladle into hot sterilized jars. Adjust lids and screw bands and process in a boiling water bath for 5 minutes. Start counting time when water comes to a boil.
- To remake with liquid pectin: For each quart of jam, measure 3/4 cup sugar, 2 tbsp lemon juice, and 2 tbsp liquid pectin. Bring the jam to boiling over high heat. Quickly add the sugar, lemon juice, and pectin and bring to a full rolling boil; stir constantly. Boil mixture hard for 1 minute. Remove from heat; skim off foam. Ladle into hot sterilized jars. Adjust lids and screw bands and process in a boiling water bath for 5 minutes. Start counting time when water comes to a boil. If there are problems with the Sure-Jell One Step (Fruit Pectin-Sugar Mix), call General Foods toll-free number, 1-800-431-1001, from 6 am to 1 pm Seattle time.
